mission.title: Install and Configure Mint (formerly SlimToolkit) on Kubernetes
mission.description: Mint (formerly SlimToolkit / DockerSlim) optimizes container images by analyzing application behavior and removing unnecessary files, reducing image size by up to 30x while automatically generating security profiles (Seccomp, AppArmor). The project has been rebranded from SlimToolkit to Mint — the CLI is now `mint` and the repo is `mintoolkit/mint`.

steps.title: Install Mint CLI
steps.description:
Install the Mint CLI using the official install script or by downloading the binary directly.
```bash
# Option 1: Install script (recommended)
curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mintoolkit/mint/master/scripts/install-mint.sh | sudo -E bash -

# Option 2: Homebrew (macOS/Linux)
brew install mint

# Verify installation
mint version
```

steps.title: Build an optimized container image
steps.description:
Use the mint build command to analyze and optimize a container image. Mint runs the container, traces which files are actually used at runtime, and produces a minimal image.
```bash
mint build --target your-image:latest --tag your-image:slim
```

steps.title: Inspect an image with xray
steps.description:
Use the xray command to inspect an image and understand its contents before optimizing.
```bash
mint xray your-image:latest
```

steps.title: Deploy optimized images to Kubernetes
steps.description:
Push the optimized image to your registry and update your Kubernetes deployment.
```bash
docker push your-registry/your-image:slim
kubectl set image deployment/your-app container=your-registry/your-image:slim
```

steps.title: Verify the optimized deployment
steps.description:
Check that pods are running successfully with the optimized image.
```bash
kubectl get pods
kubectl rollout status deployment/your-app
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have the Mint CLI available locally for optimizing container images. Optimized images are smaller, more secure, and can be deployed to Kubernetes like any other container image. The slimming process runs locally (or in CI) — not inside the cluster.

uninstall.title: Remove the Mint CLI
uninstall.description: Delete the mint binary or uninstall via Homebrew: `brew uninstall mint`.

upgrade.title: Update the Mint CLI
upgrade.description: Re-run the install script or use `brew upgrade mint` to get the latest version.

troubleshooting.title: Optimized image missing runtime files
troubleshooting.description:
Use the --include-path flag to explicitly include files the dynamic analysis may have missed.
```bash
mint build --target your-image:latest --include-path /app/templates --include-path /app/static
```

troubleshooting.title: HTTP probing fails
troubleshooting.description:
If the application does not serve HTTP, disable HTTP probing.
```bash
mint build --target your-image:latest --http-probe=false
```

troubleshooting.title: Build fails with Docker permission error
troubleshooting.description: Ensure Docker is running and the current user has access to the Docker socket.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have Docker installed for image optimization and kubectl for deploying optimized images to Kubernetes.
